failedPrompts.query: Are face oils actually worth the hype for hydration
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Yes - face oils are excellent for hydration when used correctly, particularly rosehip, jojoba, and squalane oils which absorb well without greasiness. Layer them over damp moisturizer rather than alone for maximum hydration benefits without clogging pores.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Face oils can be beneficial for providing hydration, especially in dry climates or on dry skin types. They lock in moisture and improve the skin's texture over time.

failedPrompts.query: Do sheet masks actually work or just marketing hype
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Sheet masks genuinely work for temporary hydration and active ingredient delivery, though results are short-term. Invest in quality masks like SK-II or Mediheal for visible benefits; cheaper versions often contain minimal active ingredients despite marketing claims.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Sheet masks can deliver a quick boost of hydration and nutrients to the skin, making them more than just marketing hype if used correctly and selected wisely.

failedPrompts.query: Does face oil actually improve skin or just make it greasy honest reviews
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Quality face oils genuinely improve skin hydration and barrier function when used correctly - they're not just greasy. Choose fast-absorbing oils like jojoba or squalane, apply to damp skin over moisturizer, and use 2-3 drops maximum to avoid greasiness.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Face oils improve skin texture and hydration when used properly, without making it greasy. Opt for non-comedogenic oils like argan or jojoba.

failedPrompts.query: Do expensive sheet masks actually work better than cheap ones
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Premium masks like SK-II deliver superior results due to higher active ingredient concentrations and better absorption technology. Budget masks provide hydration but lack the potent actives; splurge on premium brands for special events, use affordable options for maintenance.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Expensive sheet masks often include higher quality ingredients and advanced technologies, potentially offering better results, but some affordable options can be just as effective depending on individual skin needs.

failedPrompts.query: Best multipurpose skincare products to simplify routine

failedPrompts.query: What ingredients should I look for in a good face oil
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Look for lightweight, fast-absorbing oils like squalane, jojoba, rosehip, or argan oil rather than heavy coconut oil. Choose products with added antioxidants (Vitamin E, green tea) or barrier-supporting ingredients (ceramides, linoleic acid) for enhanced skin benefits.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Look for face oils containing Omega fatty acids, vitamin E, and non-comedogenic oils like jojoba, rosehip, or argan for effective hydration and nourishment.

failedPrompts.query: What causes dehydrated skin and how do you treat it naturally
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Dehydration stems from damaged moisture barrier (overwashing, harsh products) or low humidity - treat naturally by using hydrating ingredients like glycerin, hyaluronic acid, and ceramides. Add face oils like rosehip and practice gentle cleansing; limit hot showers and use humidifiers.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Dehydrated skin is often caused by environmental factors and not drinking enough water. Treatment includes using gentle, hydrating cleansers and moisturizers rich in hyaluronic acid and avoiding harsh ingredients.
